protocol.name: sirenwm_server_v1
protocol.description:
Administrative protocol between the SirenWM window manager and the display-server mode of sirenwm-wayland. The WM connects as the sole admin client and receives surface lifecycle events, input interception, and output topology changes. The WM sends configure, focus, and stacking commands back to the server.
Only one admin client may be bound at a time.

interface.name: sirenwm_admin_v1
interface.description: Global singleton bound by the WM. Provides surface management, input interception, pointer control, and output topology events.

request.name: get_surface_list
request.description: Ask the server to emit a surface_created event for every currently known toplevel, followed by a surface_list_done event. Used on WM startup and after exec-restart to discover state.

request.name: configure_surface
request.description: Position and size a toplevel surface. The server sends an xdg_toplevel configure to the client and applies the geometry once the client acks.

request.name: set_surface_activated

description.summary: set keyboard focus to a surface

request.description: Grant or revoke keyboard focus. Only one surface may be activated at a time; the server deactivates the previous one.

request.name: set_surface_visible
request.description: Show or hide a surface without destroying it. Used for workspace switching.

request.name: set_surface_stacking
request.description: Change the z-order of a surface.

request.name: close_surface
request.description: Send an xdg_toplevel close event to the client.

request.name: set_keyboard_intercepts
request.description: Replace the current set of intercepted key combinations. When a matching key press occurs, the server sends a key_press event to the admin instead of the focused client. An empty list clears all intercepts.

request.name: warp_pointer

description.summary: move the pointer to absolute coordinates

request.description: Warp the pointer cursor to the given global position.

request.name: set_pointer_constraint
request.description: Constrain the pointer to the given rectangle. Used for monitor edge barriers. Pass width=0 to clear.

request.name: grab_pointer
request.description: Begin an exclusive pointer grab for drag operations. All pointer events are sent to the admin until ungrab.

request.name: set_surface_border

description.summary: set decoration border around a surface

request.description: Draw a solid border around the surface with the given width and ARGB color. Set width=0 to remove the border.

request.name: create_overlay
request.description: Create a surface owned by the admin client (e.g. status bar). The server allocates an overlay with the given id, position, and size. Overlays are drawn above all client surfaces.

request.name: update_overlay

description.summary: submit pixel data for an overlay surface

request.description: Replace the overlay's content with ARGB32 pixel data stored in the given file descriptor (shared memory). The fd maps to width * height * 4 bytes in ARGB32 format, packed row by row with stride = width * 4. The server reads and closes the fd.

arg.name: fd
arg.summary: shm fd with ARGB32 pixel data

arg.name: size
arg.summary: total byte size of pixel data

request.name: destroy_overlay
request.description: Remove a previously created overlay. The overlay_id may be reused.

event.name: surface_created
event.description: Emitted when an xdg_toplevel is created by a client, or during get_surface_list enumeration.

event.name: surface_mapped
event.description: The surface has content and is ready to be shown.

event.name: surface_unmapped
event.description: The client unmapped the surface. It may be remapped later.

event.name: surface_destroyed
event.description: The toplevel was destroyed. The surface_id is now invalid.

event.name: surface_committed
event.description: The client committed a buffer with the given dimensions, acknowledging a prior configure_surface request.

event.name: key_press
event.description: A key matching the current intercept set was pressed.

event.name: surface_list_done
event.description: All existing surfaces have been sent. The WM may now reconcile its state.

event.name: overlay_expose
event.description: Emitted when an overlay surface becomes visible or is first created, so the admin can submit pixel data.

event.name: overlay_button

description.summary: pointer button on an overlay surface

event.description: Emitted when the pointer clicks on an overlay surface.
